issues: No background hero image — the entire hero is a flat black void with zero atmospheric depth, unlike the good reference which uses a full-bleed atmospheric scene
No character art, portraits, or any imagery whatsoever — a game fan page without visuals feels like a placeholder skeleton
No film grain, noise texture, or any depth layering — the background is pure flat black (#000 or near), which breaks the immersion requirement
Data completeness is essentially zero — only the hero section exists; no character cards, weapon detail sections, stat bars, or world content is visible in this screenshot
Color system relies on only black + gold/yellow — while gold is appropriate for FF8's aesthetic, there are no surface colors, gradient depth, or secondary accents to create visual richness
The 'VIII' gold treatment is nice but the overall page feels like a landing page wireframe, not a shipped premium companion site
No glow effects, subtle shadows on elevated elements, or layered depth — everything is completely flat
The 'EXPLORE NOW' button border in the nav is slightly too prominent and the CTA buttons below feel heavy with solid gold fill
The metadata strip at the bottom (JRPG · ACTION RPG · PLAYSTATION...) is barely legible against the black background
No active nav indicator, no hover state cues visible, no card interactions or modal triggers — interactivity is absent
strengths: The two-tone heading treatment — white 'FINAL FANTASY' with gold 'VIII' — is genuinely effective and shows intentional design thinking
Typography choices feel bold and editorial; the ultra-heavy weight on the headline creates strong size contrast
The 'FAN ARCHIVE – RESTORED EDITION' badge pill is a nice detail that adds authenticity and polish
The metadata strip at the bottom (composer credit, platforms, play time) is a thoughtful content addition that elevates it above generic templates
The scroll indicator mouse icon at the bottom is a good UX touch consistent with the reference examples
Clean nav structure with appropriate spacing and spaced-caps treatment
Dual CTA button approach (filled vs outlined) correctly distinguishes primary from secondary actions
specific_improvements: Add a full-bleed atmospheric background image behind the hero — for FF8, this could be Balamb Garden exterior, the moon, or a stylized Squall/Rinoa silhouette with a dark blue/purple overlay tint to preserve text legibility
Add a film grain / noise texture CSS overlay (e.g., SVG feTurbulence or a PNG grain overlay at 5-8% opacity) to create the cinematic immersion that separates fan sites from generic pages

Build out character card section below the hero — large portrait images (Squall, Rinoa, Seifer, Quistis) with stat bars for STR/MAG/SPD/HP and their signature GF/weapon, matching the reference card grid exactly
Add a weapons section with large detail art showing iconic FF8 weapons (Revolver gunblade, Hyperion, etc.) with stats below each
Introduce a subtle surface color — replace pure black with zinc-950 (#09090b) and add zinc-900 cards to create depth layering between background, surface, and elevated elements
Add a radial glow or atmospheric mist effect behind the 'VIII' text in gold — a soft amber/gold radial gradient at low opacity would create the glowing logo effect appropriate for the aesthetic
Change the primary CTA button from solid gold to a gold-bordered glass button with gold text to feel more premium and less like a standard Bootstrap button
Add a 'WORLD' section with a stylized FF8 world map or key locations (Balamb, Galbadia, Esthar) with descriptive cards, mirroring the world exploration section in the references
Make the metadata strip more legible by bumping opacity from ~40% to 65% and adding a thin gold separator between each item instead of a dot
Add a Lore/GF (Guardian Forces) section featuring summon cards with junction system information to demonstrate data completeness and content depth
